scenario: Atlas Marketplace operates a Camunda 8.8 Tasklist deployment serving 7000 tasks per day. A product manager asks what an agent sees on the right-hand detail panel after picking a task from the queue, particularly for user tasks where the BPMN modeler did not attach a Camunda Form to the element before deploying the process model.
question: What does the Tasklist task detail view display when the selected task has no attached form in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The detail view renders a generic completion form with one text input per BPMN attribute so the agent can capture freeform notes before completing
The detail view redirects to Operate's process instance page and the agent completes the task by editing variables in the audit panel there
The detail view displays the task variables so the agent can review their values and complete the task without filling out a Camunda Form
The detail view shows an empty placeholder and the task cannot be completed from Tasklist until a Camunda Form is attached and the process is redeployed

a.text: Incorrect. Tasklist does not auto-generate a generic completion form from BPMN attributes. When the task has no attached form, the detail view falls back to displaying the existing task variables.

b.text: Incorrect. Tasklist does not redirect the agent to Operate. When the task has no form, the Tasklist detail panel itself displays the task variables and the agent completes the task in place.

c.text: Correct. The Tasklist user guide states that if a task does not have a form attached, the task details view displays the task variables so the agent can review them and complete the task.

d.text: Incorrect. Tasks without an attached form remain completable from Tasklist. The detail view simply shows the task variables instead of a Camunda Form, and the agent can still complete the task.

explanation: In the Tasklist detail view, a task with an attached Camunda Form shows the form to fill out and submit. If no form is attached, the panel instead displays the task variables, and the agent completes the task from that variables view.
